heading: Department of Homeland Security data framework
label: 126 Department of Homeland Security data framework
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Department of Homeland Security Data Framework Act of 2018, and not as part of the Homeland Security Act of 2002 which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Development
children.label: 1 Development
children.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security shall develop a data framework to integrate existing Department of Homeland Security datasets and systems, as appropriate, for access by authorized personnel in a manner consistent with relevant legal authorities and privacy, civil rights, and civil liberties policies and protections.

children.label: A
children.text: All information acquired, held, or obtained by an office or component of the Department of Homeland Security that falls within the scope of the information sharing environment, including homeland security information, terrorism information, weapons of mass destruction information, and national intelligence.

children.label: B
children.text: Any information or intelligence relevant to priority mission needs and capability requirements of the homeland security enterprise, as determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: have an appropriate security clearance;

children.label: B
children.text: are assigned to perform a function that requires access to information in such framework; and

children.label: C
children.text: are trained in applicable standards for safeguarding and using such information.

children.label: A
children.text: issue guidance for Department of Homeland Security employees authorized to access and contribute to the data framework pursuant to paragraph (1); and

children.label: B
children.text: ensure that such guidance enforces a duty to share between offices and components of the Department when accessing or contributing to such framework for mission needs.

children.heading: Efficiency
children.label: 3 Efficiency
children.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security shall promulgate data standards and instruct components of the Department of Homeland Security to make available information through the data framework required under this section in a machine-readable standard format, to the greatest extent practicable.
jeopardize the protection of sources, methods, or activities;
compromise a criminal or national security investigation;
be inconsistent with other Federal laws or regulations; or
be duplicative or not serve an operational purpose if included in such framework.
Mechanisms for identifying insider threats.
Mechanisms for identifying security risks.
Safeguards for privacy, civil rights, and civil liberties.

children.heading: Deadline for implementation
children.label: e Deadline for implementation
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Secretary of Homeland Security shall ensure the data framework required under this section has the ability to include appropriate information in existence within the Department of Homeland Security to meet the critical mission operations of the Department of Homeland Security.December 19, 2018

children.heading: Status updates
children.label: 1 Status updates
children.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security shall submit to the appropriate congressional committees regular updates on the status of the data framework until the framework is fully operational.

children.heading: Operational notification
children.label: 2 Operational notification
children.text: Not later than 60 days after the date on which the data framework required under this section is fully operational, the Secretary of Homeland Security shall provide notice to the appropriate congressional committees that the data framework is fully operational.

children.heading: Value added
children.label: 3 Value added
children.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security shall annually brief Congress on component use of the data framework required under this section to support operations that disrupt terrorist activities and incidents in the homeland.

children.heading: Appropriate congressional committee; homeland
children.label: 1 Appropriate congressional committee; homeland
children.text: The terms “appropriate congressional committee” and “homeland” have the meaning given those terms in .section 101 of this title

children.heading: Homeland security information
children.label: 2 Homeland security information
children.text: The term “homeland security information” has the meaning given such term in .section 482 of this title

children.heading: National intelligence
children.label: 3 National intelligence
children.text: The term “national intelligence” has the meaning given such term in .section 3003(5) of title 50

children.heading: Terrorism information
children.label: 4 Terrorism information
children.text: The term “terrorism information” has the meaning given such term in .section 485 of this title
